goal: Build a small Python utility as a team. Domain is open — pick anything useful in under 200 lines. Must include workspace/README.md and a smoke test that actually runs. Prefer the standard library. The utility should be offline-friendly (no network calls in normal operation). The reviewer will inspect every code artifact.

tasks.note: 195-line log analyser + smoke test. All 17 tests pass, exit 0. stdlib only (argparse, re, collections, pathlib). Offline, no network calls. Smoke test covers classify_line, analyse_file (no filter, level filter, keyword, combined), and main() exit codes + flags.

README written after reading logalyzer.py and test_smoke.py. Covers all six required sections: title/description, features, requirements, installation, usage (four CLI examples with plausible output), test command, and MIT license. Caveats section added for encoding, first-match-wins classification, and no tail-mode.

All review_v1.md issues addressed. Blocking: README --level example corrected (path b) to show full distribution summary + filtered lines. Non-blocking 1: removed dead show_lines param from analyse_file/analyse_files. Non-blocking 2: added --level validation with helpful error message. Non-blocking 3: FAILURES.clear() at main_runner() entry. 19 tests pass, exit 0. 197 lines.
Fixed four arithmetic/formatting errors in the examples. No structural or tonal changes made.
